whatWasImplemented: src/lib/files/parsers.ts — parseFile(bytes, ext) dispatcher. PDF via pdf-parse v2 (PDFParse class, data param); DOCX via mammoth.extractRawText (strips VBA macros by design — mammoth ignores /word/vbaProject.bin); PPTX via officeparser v5 parseOfficeAsync; XLSX via xlsx package with sheet-named section headers; CSV/TXT/MD via TextDecoder with fatal=true -> fatal=false fallback (graceful non-UTF8 handling per VAL-FILE-023).
isEncryptedPdf heuristic — scans first 4KB for /Encrypt marker, throws FileParseError('pdf_encrypted', ...) early for VAL-FILE-020.

MAX_EXTRACTED_BYTES = 100MB — every parser calls assertBelowCap post-extraction. Zip-bomb (VAL-FILE-016) + PDF-bomb (VAL-FILE-017) defense: a tiny input that decompresses to 5GB triggers the throw because the extracted text exceeds 100MB before the rest of the pipeline sees it.
FileParseError carries a stable code + user-readable message so the UI can show retryable vs terminal errors.

src/lib/files/process.ts — processFile(fileId) orchestrator: load row (service role) -> status=processing -> Storage.download -> parseFile -> chunkMarkdown (reused from crawl pipeline) -> OpenAI embed in batches of 100 with p-limit(3) -> embeddings INSERT with source_type='file' + file_id FK + source_url='file://<filename>' -> status=ready with chunks_count. Any thrown error (FileParseError or otherwise) transitions the row to status=failed with error_message and returns without rethrowing.

src/app/api/files/process/route.ts — POST {file_id} wrapper. Primary invocation path is after() from the upload route; this route exposes the same processor for a future 'Retry' button on failed files (M5F6 UI).
src/app/api/files/route.ts — after(processFile(fileId)) hook after response. Failures in the hook are swallowed and logged; the row still reflects status=failed from process.ts own error branch.

src/__tests__/files-parsers.test.ts — 12 parser tests: TextDecoder strict/fallback, encrypted PDF detection, real PDF via pdf-lib construction + pdf-parse round-trip, XLSX multi-sheet with aoa_to_sheet, CSV + TXT + MD.

src/__tests__/files-process.test.ts — 5 orchestration tests: TXT happy path with queued -> processing -> ready transitions (VAL-FILE-014), embedding rows shape (source_type='file', file_id, source_url), encrypted PDF routes to failed, empty-extracted-text routes to failed, Storage download error routes to failed.
Dependencies added: pdf-parse@2, officeparser@5, mammoth, xlsx.
whatWasLeftUndone: Real zip-bomb + PDF-bomb fixtures would validate MAX_EXTRACTED_BYTES end-to-end against the actual parsers, not just the assertion wrapper. Deferred to adversarial-fixture pass; the wrapper is unit-covered.

Real DOCX-with-macro + real encrypted PDF + real PPTX fixtures to smoke VAL-FILE-022 / 020 / 005 fully end-to-end. Logic paths covered via targeted byte buffers + the parser dispatcher; full adversarial fixtures would go into src/__tests__/fixtures/ during Playwright pass.
Browser-side 'Retry' trigger (VAL-FILE-015) — the API endpoint exists; UI lands in M5F6.
discoveredIssues: officeparser v6/v7 switched to an AST API with disk-only file input. Pinned to v5.2.2 which still accepts Buffer/ArrayBuffer via parseOfficeAsync. Revisit during M8 hardening if v5 falls out of support.
pdf-parse v2 uses `new PDFParse({data: bytes}).getText()` + requires .destroy() — called in a finally block to free pdfjs-dist resources between uploads.

Tests that import /api/files/route.ts need to mock `next/server` -> after + @/lib/files/process -> processFile; otherwise after() is called with a real async function and Vitest reports stray mock pollution. Added both to file-upload.test.ts.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 418 passed / 1 skipped / 55 files (+17 new: 12 parser + 5 process).

manualChecks.action: Real PDF bytes via pdf-lib + pdf-parse round-trip

manualChecks.expected: decode with replacement char, no throw
